# UC-22.15.50 · DMZ Integrity Between IT and OT — Corporate Browser to OT HMI

## Description

Finds IT-originated web traffic reaching OT HMIs that should only be reachable via bastion or reverse proxy in the DMZ.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes fw and sourcetypes pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Enforce explicit proxy paths; (2) split DNS views; (3) microsegment HMI; (4) user awareness for bookmarked IPs. Indexes required: `index=fw`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`; Lookups: `ot_hmi_exposure`, `ot_hmi_exposure.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Palo Alto (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:traffic` url_category, zones.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enforce explicit proxy paths; (2) split DNS views; (3) microsegment HMI; (4) user awareness for bookmarked IPs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-7d
| where dest_zone="OT-Web-HMI" AND src_zone="Corporate-Users" AND app="web-browsing"
| lookup ot_hmi_exposure.csv dest_ip OUTPUT exposure
| where exposure!="via_dmz_proxy"
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, url_category, user
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**DMZ Integrity Between IT and OT — Corporate Browser to OT HMI (IEC 62443-3-2 / DMZ integrity between IT and OT)** — Finds IT-originated web traffic reaching OT HMIs that should only be reachable via bastion or reverse proxy in the DMZ.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic` url_category, zones. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: fw; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=fw, sourcetype="pan:traffic", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where dest_zone="OT-Web-HMI" AND src_zone="Corporate-Users" AND app="web-browsing"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where exposure!="via_dmz_proxy"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_ip, dest_ip, url_category, user** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**DMZ Integrity Between IT and OT — Corporate Browser to OT HMI (IEC 62443-3-2 / DMZ integrity between IT and OT)** — Finds IT-originated web traffic reaching OT HMIs that should only be reachable via bastion or reverse proxy in the DMZ.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic` url_category, zones. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Web.Web` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Map (src), Time chart.

## SPL

```spl
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-7d
| where dest_zone="OT-Web-HMI" AND src_zone="Corporate-Users" AND app="web-browsing"
| lookup ot_hmi_exposure.csv dest_ip OUTPUT exposure
| where exposure!="via_dmz_proxy"
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, url_category, user
| sort - count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table, Map (src), Time chart. Key fields: `dest_ip`, `src_ip`, `url_category`, `user`.
